Title: A flavonoid 3-O-glucoside:2″-O-glucosyltransferase responsible for terminal modification of pollen-specific flavonols in Arabidopsis thaliana

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PHD-finger transcription factor with Leu zipper-like motif
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Associated with tapetum development and pollen wall synthesis during anther development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ms1 mutants lack the pollen-specific flavonols f21 and f26, indicating MS1 is required upstream for their accumulation, likely via tapetum/pollen development and/or gene expression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant metabolite profiling comparing ms1 and wild type
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ms1 mutants were used to identify pollen-specific flavonols absent when pollen/tapetum development is defective. 2. Loss of MS1 correlates with absence of f21 and f26. 3. MS1 is inferred to act upstream of UGT79B6/pollen flavonol accumulation, but direct regulation was not shown.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutants show severe male sterility, defective pollen wall formation, and degeneration of microspores and tapetum
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited value for nutrient engineering because it is essential for reproductive development; useful as an upstream developmental regulator marker for studying pollen flavonol biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray comparisons of ms1 versus wild type cited and used to infer downstream genes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although MS1 is a transcription factor, in this paper it serves mainly as an upstream developmental background gene used to infer UGT79B6, and direct regulation of flavonol genes was not demonstrated

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; Ler; Col er105 (Big Mama background for TILLING lines)
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase (UGT79 family); PSPG box-containing glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Flavonoid biosynthesis; flavonol glycoside terminal modification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose mainly from UDP-glucose to the 2″-hydroxyl of the sugar moiety attached at flavonoid 3-O position, converting flavonoid 3-O-glucosides into 3-O-glucosyl-(1→2)-glucosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pollen-associated flavonols; physiological role in Arabidopsis fertility not established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly in early pollen developmental stages; abundant in floral buds; localized in tapetum cells and microspores of anthers at stages 8–11; nearly undetectable in stems and siliques
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Abundant in floral buds, specifically developing anthers/tapetum and microspores
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for accumulation of pollen-specific flavonol 3-O-diglucosides; knockout abolishes f21 and f26 in flowers/pollen-related tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TILLING knockout mutants; complementation with genomic UGT79B6; recombinant protein enzyme assay; metabolite profiling by UPLC/PDA/ESI/Q-TOF/MS; qRT-PCR; promoter-GUS localization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT79B6 was identified from ms1-dependent transcriptome clues as the candidate pollen flavonol glycosyltransferase. 2. Independent ugt79b6 nonsense mutants lost kaempferol/quercetin 3-O-glucosyl-(1→2)-glucosides. 3. Genomic complementation restored the wild-type flavonol profile. 4. Recombinant UGT79B6 converted kaempferol 3-O-glucoside to the corresponding 2″-O-glucosylated diglucoside and strongly preferred UDP-glucose. 5. UGT79B6 is expressed specifically in tapetum cells and microspores of developing anthers.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal modification enzyme for determining pollen-specific flavonol structure; not established as pathway rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ugt79b6 mutants remained fertile with no obvious mature pollen morphology defect; one TILLING line had unrelated sterility/morphological defects due to background EMS mutations
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering pollen-specific flavonol glycosylation patterns and studying reproductive flavonoid specialization; likely more valuable as a pathway tailoring gene than for agronomic improvement because loss caused little obvious fertility defect in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray analysis comparing wild type and ms1 mutants (cited from prior studies); untargeted metabolomics/UPLC-PDA-MS in this study
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Accepts 3-O-glucosylated and some 3-O-galactosylated flavonoids/anthocyanins; does not use 3,5- or 3,7-diglycosylated flavonoids efficiently, suggesting C-5/C-7 glycosylation occurs after full C-3 modification; promoter region contains predicted MYB-binding sites
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native UGT79B6 promoter fragments (683 bp and 2000 bp) for complementation/GUS; endogenous promoter, tissue-specific
